repentance-prayer.title: Prayer of Repentance
repentance-prayer.by: Written by Ruby Rose and recited and produced by Suno
repentance-prayer.text:
Father in heaven, (Matthew 6:9)
I come before You with empty hands and a shattered heart.
I have sinned against You. (Psalm 51:4)
I have turned my back on Your goodness and chosen my own dark way. (Isaiah 53:6)

My sin is ever before me — it tears at my soul and leaves me in agony. (Psalm 51:3)
I groan and weep under the unbearable weight I created for myself.
Have mercy on me, O God, according to Your unfailing love.

Blot out my transgressions.
Wash me thoroughly from my iniquity
and cleanse me from my sin. (Psalm 51:1-2)

I am sorry, Father — I am so deeply sorry.
I am sorry for the doubt that poisoned my mind and made me question Your love.
I am sorry for the sin I kept choosing even when it destroyed me.

I am sorry for the anger that raged inside me and wounded everyone around me.
I am sorry for the running — running from Your presence,
running from Your truth, running until I was completely lost and empty.

I am sorry for the pain I caused — the pain in Your heart,
the pain I brought into the lives of those I love,
and the crushing pain I brought upon my own soul.

Jesus, my Lord and Savior,
remember me in this brokenness. (Luke 23:42)
Forgive me.
Wash me clean in Your blood. (1 John 1:7; Revelation 1:5)
Make me whole again.

Create in me a clean heart, O God,
and renew a right spirit within me.
Do not cast me away from Your presence.
Restore to me the joy of Your salvation
and uphold me with Your Spirit. (Psalm 51:10-12)

I am Yours, Father.
I turn back to You now with tears and trembling.
I have nowhere else to go. (John 6:68)
Receive me, hold me, heal me, and make me new.

For Yours is the kingdom,
Yours is the power,
and Yours is the glory,
forever and ever. (Matthew 6:13)

In Jesus' name I pray,
Amen.

family-prayer.title: Prayer for Family
family-prayer.by: Written by Ruby Rose and recited and produced by Suno
family-prayer.text:
God of heaven and earth,
my Father, here I am again,
heart heavy, lifting up to You the people I love the most.
The ones whose smiles light up my darkest days,
whose pain I can feel even when they try to hide it.

Your name is holy... so holy. (Matthew 6:9; Isaiah 6:3)
You're the God who stitches families together,
who created us and calls us Your own kids. (John 1:12; Ephesians 3:15)
You're a Father to the ones who never had one, (Psalm 68:5)
a defender for the hurting and the left-behind. (Psalm 68:5)
You love them even more than I do.
So I'm laying them down at Your feet right now...
because I can't do this alone.
I can't carry it all. (1 Peter 5:7; Matthew 11:28)

Jesus, Lord... watch over my spouse;
the one You prepared for me.
Guard our hearts when they get tired or angry. (Philippians 4:7)
Teach us to forgive, to hold on tight, (Matthew 6:14; Colossians 3:13)
to laugh when things feel heavy.
Make our home a safe place where Your peace surrounds us,
where You're close and real. (Psalm 127:1; John 14:27)

Lord, my kids... the ones You've put in my care;
cover them, please. Wrap them in Your arms of love. (Psalm 91:4; Deuteronomy 33:27)
Even when I'm there walking beside them, be their guiding light. (Psalm 119:105; John 8:12)
Give them hearts that run after You. (Psalm 119:10)
Block the lies and darkness that try to enter their mind. (2 Corinthians 10:5)
Keep their bodies safe from accidents and illness. (Psalm 91:10-11)
Guard their souls from anything that tries to pull them away from You, Lord. (John 10:28-29)

My parents... the ones who worried for me when I was young,
who carried loads I didn't even see until I got older.
Touch them with real strength.
Give them health that lasts, peace that settles deep, and dignity in every season. (Isaiah 46:4)
Where we've hurt each other or grown distant, bring healing, real healing. (Psalm 147:3)
Help me honor them the way You said, even when it's hard. (Exodus 20:12)

My brothers, sisters, and the rest of my family;
I lift every single one up to You.
If anyone's sick or in need of You, touch them.
Heal what's breaking. (Psalm 103:3; James 5:14-15)
If they're fighting battles I can't even name, stand and fight for them. (Exodus 14:14)
If they're lost and wandering, chase them down and bring them home. (Luke 15:4-7)
If they're lonely in a crowded room, send arms to hold them,
someone to sit in the silence with them. (Psalm 68:6)

Father... the one thing I want most of all for them is You.
If any of them don't know You yet, don't let them stay lost.
Run after them. (Luke 15:4-7; 2 Peter 3:9)
Soften the hard places in their hearts that need You. (Ezekiel 36:26)
Send the right person, the right word, the right moment to show them Jesus.
Protect them when they're driving down dark roads.
Protect them when they lay their heads down at night and worries creep in. (Psalm 121:7-8; Psalm 4:8)
Protect them from the enemy who wants to steal their joy, kill their hope, and destroy what's good. (John 10:10)
Send Your angels to camp around their houses, around their cars, around every step they take. (Psalm 91:11; Psalm 34:7)

Pull us together, Lord.
Where we're divided and distant with each other, melt these walls with Your love. (Ephesians 4:3; John 17:21)
Where words have stopped and walls went up, give us courage to talk real again.
Where fear has moved in, kick it out and fill the space with trust and love. (1 John 4:18)
Let our family be a picture of how You keep Your promises and never let go. (Deuteronomy 7:9; Hebrews 10:23)
I can't control what they choose.
But I can bring every name, every tear, every unspoken fear straight to You...
and I trust You, Father;
I trust in Your will and Your love. (Proverbs 3:5-6)

For Yours is the kingdom,
Yours is the power,
and Yours is the glory,
forever and ever. (Matthew 6:13)

In Jesus' name; my Savior, my King. (Philippians 2:9-11)
Amen.

loneliness-prayer.title: Prayer of Loneliness
loneliness-prayer.by: Written by Ruby Rose and recited and produced by Suno
loneliness-prayer.text:
Father in heaven,
God who sees every hidden heart,
I come to You just as I am — lonely, tired, and unseen.

Father, people are all around me — at work, at the store, even in my own home —
but I feel invisible.
No one really sees me.
No one really knows the ache inside.

I smile and say the right things,
but inside I’m screaming for someone to truly see me and stay.
I’m so tired of pretending I’m okay when I feel this empty.
I don’t even want the phone to ring anymore, Lord.
I don’t know what to say without feeling like I’m faking everything.
So I stay quiet… and the silence gets louder and louder.

The nights are the hardest.
I lie there while the hours move so slowly,
dreading the morning because it means I have to get up and do it all over again —
smile, pretend, feel alone in a room full of people.
My heart literally hurts, Father.
It aches to be wanted. It aches to be known. It aches to matter to someone.

Oh Lord, turn to me and be gracious to me,
for I am lonely and afflicted. (Psalm 25:16)
My heart feels crushed under the weight of this emptiness.
I feel like a lonely sparrow on the housetop —
watching everyone else have someone, while I sit here alone. (Psalm 102:7)

Jesus, You know this feeling better than anyone.
You were despised and rejected by men, (Isaiah 53:3)
a man of sorrows and acquainted with grief.
Even Your closest friends deserted You in Your darkest hour. (Matthew 26:56; Mark 14:50)
You know what it’s like to be surrounded by people and still feel completely alone.
So I’m asking You — please come close to me right now. (James 4:8)

Father, I can’t carry this loneliness anymore.
It presses on my chest and makes it hard to breathe.
I need to feel loved again.
I need to feel like I matter to someone.
I need to know I’m not invisible to You.

Please break through this darkness.
Remind me that You see me when no one else does. (Psalm 139:1-3)
Hold me when I feel like no one wants to hold me. (Deuteronomy 33:27)
Love me when I feel so unloved.
Be with me in this empty place so I don’t have to be alone anymore.
I don’t have the strength to pretend anymore.

Oh Lord, I just need You.
You have said, “I will never leave you nor forsake you.” (Hebrews 13:5; Deuteronomy 31:6)
You are a friend who sticks closer than a brother. (Proverbs 18:24)
Even if my father and mother forsake me,
You will take me in. (Psalm 27:10)

At times when everyone else walked away, You stood by me. (2 Timothy 4:16-17)
You are near to the brokenhearted
and save those who are crushed in spirit. (Psalm 34:18)
You take the lonely and give them a home. (Psalm 68:6)
Father, I’m not asking for everything to change overnight.
I’m just asking You to be with me in this.

To sit with me in the silence.
To remind my heart that I am seen, I am known, and I am not alone.
Because You are with me…
I don’t have to be alone anymore.

For Yours is the kingdom,
Yours is the power,
and Yours is the glory,
forever and ever. (Matthew 6:13)

In Jesus’ name I pray,
Amen.

thankfulness-prayer.title: Prayer of Thankfulness
thankfulness-prayer.by: Written by Ruby Rose and recited and produced by Suno
thankfulness-prayer.text:
God of heaven and earth,
my loving Father in heaven,
how majestic and holy is Your name! (Psalm 8:1; Matthew 6:9; Isaiah 6:3)

You're the Almighty Creator, (Genesis 1:1)
the Faithful Redeemer,
the Good Shepherd, (John 10:11)
the Light of the World, (John 8:12)
King of kings and Lord of lords. (1 Timothy 6:15)
I rejoice in who You are!

Thank You for the magnificent things You've done!
You saved Noah and his family through the flood, (Genesis 6–9)
You delivered Moses and Your people through the Red Sea. (Exodus 14)

You gave David victory over Goliath and established his throne. (1 Samuel 17)
You protected Daniel in the lions’ den. (Daniel 6)
You led Your people into the Promised Land,
flowing with milk and honey. (Exodus 3:8; Joshua)

You sent Jesus — born of a virgin, healer of the sick,
who died for our sins on the cross,
and rose victorious from the grave. (Isaiah 7:14; Acts 10:38; 1 Corinthians 15:3-4)

You poured out mercy and grace on me,
You fill my life with good things every single day. (Psalm 103:2-5; James 1:17)
My heart overflows with joy, because of Your faithfulness!

Father, You created me with tender care,
You breathed Your own life into my being. (Genesis 2:7; Psalm 139:13-14)
Father, You watch over me day and night,
You protect me with Your mighty hand. (Psalm 121:3-8; Psalm 91)

Father, You wipe away my tears when they fall,
You carry me when I cannot stand. (Revelation 21:4; Isaiah 40:11; Deuteronomy 33:27)
In every joy and every trial,
You hold me close — my faithful Lord.

Your kingdom is coming and Your will is perfect. (Matthew 6:10)
I delight in surrendering my life to You.
Fill me with the hope and contentment that only You can give.

Thank You for my daily bread — (Matthew 6:11)
for every blessing, every sunrise, every moment of peace.
You satisfy my soul and make me glad!

You have delivered me from darkness into wonderful light. (1 Peter 2:9; Colossians 1:13)
My heart sings with gratitude, for Your constant protection and love.

I am content in You, Lord. (Philippians 4:11-13)
You are my greatest treasure and my endless joy. (Matthew 13:44)

For Yours is the kingdom,
Yours is the power,
and Yours is the glory,
forever and ever. (Matthew 6:13)

In Jesus’ name I pray,
Amen.

healing-prayer.title: Prayer of Healing
healing-prayer.by: Written by Ruby Rose and recited and produced by Suno
healing-prayer.text:
Father in heaven,
God who sees every hidden hurt,
I come to You right now — just as I am.
Broken, wounded, and in need of healing.
I need healing only You can give.

You are Jehovah Rapha — the Lord who heals. (Exodus 15:26)
You blot out transgressions and remember sins no more. (Isaiah 43:25)
You forgive sins and heal diseases. (Psalm 103:3)
You rescue out of the pit and the miry clay,
and You set feet on solid ground again. (Psalm 40:2; Psalm 103:4)

Father, Your Word declares:
"You're near when hearts are broken,
and You save those whose spirit is crushed. (Psalm 34:18)
You catch every single tear that falls,
and You know every sleepless night. (Psalm 56:8)
You sent Your Word to heal —
and You rescue from the grave." (Psalm 107:20)

Lord, Your Word also promises:
"By the stripes of Jesus I am healed, (Isaiah 53:5; 1 Peter 2:24)
and He carried my sicknesses and took my pain on Himself." (Matthew 8:17)
Father, I cry out to You with the promise of Your Word:
Heal me, Lord, and I will be healed.
Save me, and I will be saved. (Jeremiah 17:14)
Restore me to health and heal my wounds. (Jeremiah 30:17)

Jesus — Great Physician,
You never turned away anyone, who reached for You in faith.
You healed the woman who touched Your garment in desperation. (Mark 5:34)
You spoke life over Jairus’ daughter, and raised her up. (Mark 5:41-42)
You forgave and healed the man lowered through the roof. (Mark 2:5, 11-12)

Lord, I’m reaching for You right now.
Touch me with that same mercy and power.
I believe You're still the same God who heals today —
in bodies, in hearts, in minds, and in the deepest places no one else can touch.
Renew my strength like the eagle’s wings —
let me rise up and not grow weary. (Isaiah 40:31)
Let rivers of living water flow through every dry and weary place inside me. (John 7:38)
Breathe new life into me, Father.

Father, I lay it all down before You —
my body, my pain, my fears, my future.
Do what only You can do.
Restore me — not just to survive,
but to live again with joy, strength, and purpose in You.
Let Your healing rest on me and make me whole.

For Yours is the kingdom,
Yours is the power,
and Yours is the glory,
forever and ever. (Matthew 6:13)

In Jesus’ name I pray,
Amen.

today-prayer.title: Prayer for Today
today-prayer.by: Written by Ruby Rose and recited and produced by Suno
today-prayer.text:
God of heaven and earth,
Creator of the stars above and the ground beneath my feet, (Genesis 1:1; Psalm 19:1)
I come before Your throne
with a heart full of love and thankfulness. (Hebrews 4:16)

Your name is holy, Lord. (Matthew 6:9)
You are worthy of all praise, honor, and glory. (Revelation 4:11)

Thank You for Your steadfast love that endures forever. (Psalm 136:1; Psalm 100:5)
Thank You for waking me up this morning
and giving me another day. (Psalm 3:5; Lamentations 3:22-23)

Thank You for Jesus,
who suffered the Cross for me. (1 Peter 2:24; Isaiah 53:5)

Who am I, O Lord, that You hear my voice. (2 Samuel 7:18)
I am unworthy.
My heart is prone to wander,
and my hands are not clean. (Isaiah 64:6; Psalm 24:4; James 4:8)

I have sinned against You
in thought, word, and deed. (1 John 1:8)
Yet here I stand, saved by grace alone. (Ephesians 2:8-9)

Have mercy on me, God of mercy.
According to the multitude of Your tender mercies,
blot out my transgressions. (Psalm 51:1)
Create in me a clean heart, O God,
and renew a right spirit within me. (Psalm 51:10)

I love You, Jesus.
You are the Lamb who was slain for my sake. (John 1:29; Revelation 5:12)
You are the risen King who lives forever. (Revelation 1:18)

Today, I need Your help more than ever.
Help me with this day, Lord.
Give me the strength to do what needs to be done. (Philippians 4:13; Isaiah 40:31)

When the path is hard and I feel like giving up,
help me to keep putting one foot in front of the other.
Help me make it through
the moments of doubt and the waves of worry.

Protect me, Father,
from every arrow the enemy shoots my way.
Protect my mind from lies,
my heart from fear,
and my body from harm. (Psalm 91:4-5)

Be my shield and my strong tower. (Psalm 18:2)
Deliver me from evil,
and lead me in the way everlasting. (Matthew 6:13; Psalm 139:24)

Show mercy, Lord, not just to me but to others.
Have mercy on those who are suffering today —
the sick, the grieving, the poor, the lonely.

Use me to be Your hands and feet to them. (Matthew 25:40)
Help me to forgive as I have been forgiven. (Matthew 6:12,14)

Give me this day what I need:
daily bread for my body, (Matthew 6:11)
living water for my soul, (John 4:14)
and fresh grace for every hour. (2 Corinthians 12:9)

I surrender my plans to You.
Let Your will be done in my life today,
on earth as it is in heaven. (Matthew 6:10)

I trust You with all my heart.
I lean not on my own understanding.
In all my ways, I acknowledge You. (Proverbs 3:5-6)

For Yours, O Lord, is the kingdom
and the power
and the glory,
forever and ever. (Matthew 6:13)

In the name of Jesus Christ, my Lord and Savior,
Amen.

wisdom-prayer.title: Prayer of Wisdom
wisdom-prayer.by: Written by Ruby Rose and recited and produced by Suno
wisdom-prayer.text:
God of heaven and earth,
my Father in heaven,
I come before You asking not for riches or fame,
but for wisdom to walk rightly before You.

Your name is holy. (Matthew 6:9; Isaiah 6:3)
You're the God who gives wisdom generously,
to all who ask without finding fault. (James 1:5)
From Your mouth come knowledge and understanding. (Proverbs 2:6)
You store up sound wisdom for the upright. (Proverbs 2:7)

The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom,
and the knowledge of the Holy One is understanding. (Proverbs 9:10)
Teach me to begin there —
in awe of who You are.

Lord, the world shouts a thousand answers,
but only Your wisdom leads to life.
Give me a discerning heart
like the one You gave Solomon
when he asked for understanding to govern Your people. (1 Kings 3:9)

I ask for wisdom to live my daily life —
to speak what's true,
to choose what's right,
and to see my circumstances through Your eyes.

Let Your wisdom from above
— pure, peaceable, gentle, open to reason,
full of mercy and good fruits — (James 3:17)
fill my mind and govern my tongue.

Guard me from the wisdom of this age
that's earthly, unspiritual, and demonic. (James 3:15)
Keep me from pride disguised as knowledge.
Keep me from foolishness dressed as freedom.

Write Your law upon my heart
so that I may not sin against You. (Psalm 119:11; Jeremiah 31:33)
Let Your Word be a lamp to my feet
and a light to my path. (Psalm 119:105)

Let the Scriptures be my counselor
and Your Holy Spirit my teacher in every decision I face —
just as You promised Your Spirit would teach me all things
and remind me of everything Jesus said. (John 14:26)

Jesus, in whom are hidden all the treasures
of wisdom and knowledge, (Colossians 2:3)
You grew in wisdom and stature
before the Father and before men. (Luke 2:52)
Make me more like You —
quick to listen, slow to speak, slow to anger. (James 1:19)

I don't know what to do,
and my path is unclear.
Help me keep my eyes on You, (2 Chronicles 20:12)
and give me understanding that comes from above,
not from my own limited sight.

Father, You said, “If any of you lacks wisdom,
let him ask of God, who gives generously to all
without finding fault, and it will be given to him.” (James 1:5)
I'm asking now with a heart that believes.
I know You'll give generously,
and I'll receive it with a grateful and obedient heart.

For Yours is the kingdom,
Yours is the power,
and Yours is the glory,
forever and ever. (Matthew 6:13)

In Jesus' name I pray,
Amen.

need-prayer.title: Prayer of Need
need-prayer.by: Written by Ruby Rose and recited and produced by Suno
need-prayer.text:
My Father in heaven,
God of heaven and earth,
I come to You right now with nothing left in me.
My heart is shattered and the pain feels too heavy to carry.
Everything around me feels like it's falling apart.

You see every tear I've cried in the dark. (Psalm 56:8)
You know the ache I can't even put into words.
How long, Father? How long does this sorrow have to last? (Psalm 13:1)
I'm drowning in this sadness and I don't know how much more I can take. (Psalm 69:1-2)

But even here, I turn my face to You.
I have nowhere else to go. (John 6:68; Psalm 73:25)
Let Your will be done in my life,
even when I don't understand it. (Matthew 6:10; Matthew 26:42)
Hold me close, Father — wrap Your everlasting arms around me. (Deuteronomy 33:27)

Jesus, my Savior,
You know what it's like to feel abandoned and in agony. (Matthew 27:46; Psalm 22:1)
You suffered for me, so I know You understand this pain. (Hebrews 4:15; Isaiah 53:4-5)
Please draw near to me right now. (James 4:8)

Heal the deep wounds inside that no one else can touch. (Psalm 147:3)
Give me strength to carry on,
peace that will let me rest,
and hope that I will laugh again. (Isaiah 40:31; John 14:27; Psalm 126:2)

Father, comfort me in this desperate place. (2 Corinthians 1:3-4)
Love me when I feel so unworthy and alone.
Help me take one breath, one painful step at a time,
trusting that You're carrying me through. (Isaiah 40:11)

Lift this burden if it's Your will,
or give me the grace to keep walking even when it hurts. (Matthew 11:28-30; 2 Corinthians 12:9)
Pull me out of this pit of despair, Lord. (Psalm 40:2)
Restore my soul and remind me that You're still good. (Psalm 23:3; Psalm 34:8)

But I will not give up, I will lay before Your feet.
I will trust in You, I will seek You.
I am Yours completely. Hold me Lord, hold me close.
I can't do this without You. (John 15:5)

For Yours is the kingdom,
Yours is the power,
and Yours is the glory,
forever and ever. (Matthew 6:13)

In Jesus' name I pray,
Amen.

dedication-prayer.title: Prayer of Dedication
dedication-prayer.by: Written by Ruby Rose and recited and produced by Suno
dedication-prayer.text:
Father in heaven,
God of all creation,
how worthy and holy is Your name. (Matthew 6:9; Revelation 4:11)

You are the Sovereign King above all kings, (1 Timothy 6:15)
the Author of life who spoke the stars into being. (Genesis 1:1; Psalm 33:6)
Your ways are higher than my ways,
Your thoughts are deeper than my thoughts. (Isaiah 55:8-9)
I stand in awe of Your glory
and bow my heart before Your throne.

Here I am, Lord — I come to serve You. (Isaiah 6:8)
I lay down my pride at Your feet this day.
I surrender my plans, my comforts, and my own best interests.
Let Your will be done in my life, not mine. (Matthew 6:10; Matthew 26:39)
Teach me to delight in doing what pleases You, (Psalm 37:4)
to walk in Your ways with a willing heart.

Father, when the flesh wars against the Spirit within me
and pride seeks to regain control, (Galatians 5:17)
remind me that You alone are Lord of all.
Give me grace to deny myself,
take up my cross daily, and follow where You lead. (Luke 9:23)

Father, Your word is a lamp to my feet and a light to my path. (Psalm 119:105)
Write Your law upon my heart
so that I may serve You with joy and gladness. (Jeremiah 31:33)
Use my hands to do Your work,
my voice to speak Your truth,
my life as an offering of worship. (Romans 12:1)
Let every step I take bring honor to Your name
and draw others closer to Your love.

Oh Lord, I wait upon You with patient trust. (Isaiah 40:31)
Even when the path is hidden or the way is hard,
I know You see the end from the beginning. (Isaiah 46:10)
Your sovereignty is my security,
Your faithfulness my steady anchor.

Here I am, Lord — send me. (Isaiah 6:8)
I am Your servant, ready to go where You send.
I am Your vessel, ready to be poured out for Your glory. (2 Timothy 2:21)
I am Yours completely — body, mind, and spirit. (1 Corinthians 6:19-20)

Jesus, You gave Yourself fully for me.
Now I give myself fully to You.
Not my will, but Yours be done. (Luke 22:42)

For Yours is the kingdom,
Yours is the power,
and Yours is the glory,
forever and ever. (Matthew 6:13)

In Jesus' name I pray,
Amen.

protection-prayer.title: Prayer of Protection
protection-prayer.by: Written by Ruby Rose and recited and produced by Suno
protection-prayer.text:
God of heaven and earth,
my Father in heaven,
I run to You today as my refuge and my fortress.

Your name is holy. (Matthew 6:9; Isaiah 6:3)
You are my shield and my exceedingly great reward. (Genesis 15:1)
You are the Lord my Shepherd —
I shall not want. (Psalm 23:1)
You are the Almighty God
who commands His angels
concerning those who fear Him,
to guard them in all their ways. (Psalm 91:11)

Lord, cover me with Your wings. (Psalm 91:4)
Hide me in the secret place of the Most High.
Let me dwell in the shelter of the Almighty
and abide under the shadow of the Shaddai. (Psalm 91:1)

Protect me from the snares of the enemy,
from the arrows that fly by day
and the terrors that walk in darkness. (Psalm 91:5-6)
Guard my going out and my coming in,
from this time forth and even forevermore. (Psalm 121:8)

Be my shield on every side.
Surround me with Your favor as with a shield. (Psalm 5:12)
Let no weapon formed against me prosper,
for my battle belongs to You. (Isaiah 54:17; 2 Chronicles 20:15)

Jesus, my Savior and my Strong Tower,
You are the One who calmed the raging sea
and walked through the storm unafraid. (Mark 4:39)
Be my peace when danger surrounds me.
Be my courage when fear knocks at my door.

Protect my mind from lies and confusion.
Protect my heart from despair and bitterness.
Protect my body from harm and illness.
Protect my home, my family, and all I love
under the canopy of Your faithful care. (Psalm 91:10)

Where I am weak, be my strength.
Where I am vulnerable, be my wall.
Where I cannot see what lies ahead,
be my light and my guide through every valley. (Psalm 23:4)

I will not be afraid of ten thousands of people
who set themselves against me,
for You, O Lord, are a shield about me —
my glory and the lifter of my head. (Psalm 3:3; Psalm 3:6)

Father, I trust You to keep what I commit to You.
You are faithful. (2 Timothy 1:12)
You will not let the righteous be moved. (Psalm 55:22)
You who keep me will neither slumber nor sleep. (Psalm 121:3-4)

For Yours is the kingdom,
Yours is the power,
and Yours is the glory,
forever and ever. (Matthew 6:13)

In Jesus' name I pray,
Amen.
